{{Quote|Is that your Podracer, or are you selling scrap metal?|Cy Yunga|translated=1|audio=Navior-taunt.flac|<ref name="E1R" />}}
'''Cy Yunga''' was an [[Cy Yunga's species|alien]] [[Podracer/Legends|Podracer]] [[Pilot/Legends|pilot]] who was active during the [[Standard year/Legends|year]] [[32 BBY/Legends|32 BBY]].<ref name="NEC1">''[[The New Essential Chronology]]'' dates the events of [[Star Wars: Episode I The Phantom Menace|''Star Wars'': Episode I ''The Phantom Menace'']] to the [[Standard year/Legends|year]] [[32 BBY/Legends|32 BBY]]. The racing season presented in the [[Star Wars video games|game]] ''[[Star Wars: Episode I Racer|Star Wars Episode I: Racer]]'' must have taken place during this year due to the appearances of [[Qui-Gon Jinn/Legends|Qui-Gon Jinn]], [[Jar Jar Binks/Legends|Jar Jar Binks]], and [[R2-D2/Legends|R2-D2]] on [[Tatooine/Legends|Tatooine]], which correspond to ''The Phantom Menace''.</ref> Yunga piloted a [[Color/Legends|red]] and white [[Cy Yunga's Podracer|Podracer]] during his career, which included at least one race on the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim]] [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Tatooine/Legends|Tatooine]]. Yunga's career also spanned to the planet [[Aquilaris]], where Yunga was considered to be one of the crowd favorites for the [[Sunken City/Legends|Sunken City]] course.

==Biography==
[[File:Cy_Yunga_Podrace.png|thumb|left|200px|Cy Yunga participated in a Podrace on Tatooine.]]
Hailing from an [[Cy Yunga's species|alien]] [[Species/Legends|species]], Cy Yunga was a [[Podracer/Legends|Podracer]] [[Pilot/Legends|pilot]]<ref name="E1R" /> during the [[Standard year/Legends|year]] [[32 BBY/Legends|32 BBY]]. Alongside fellow pilot [[Navior|"Bullseye" Navior]], Yunga was viewed as a crowd favorite for the [[Sunken City/Legends|Sunken City]] race course located on the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Aquilaris]].<ref name="E1R" />

At some point during their career, Yunga participated in a race on the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim]] planet [[Tatooine/Legends|Tatooine]]. Yunga's competitors included the [[Devlikk/Legends|Devlikk]] [[Wan Sandage/Legends|Wan Sandage]], the [[Gran/Legends|Gran]] [[Mawhonic/Legends|Mawhonic]], the [[Toong/Legends|Toong]] [[Ben Quadinaros/Legends|Ben Quadinaros]], and [[Podracing/Legends|Podrace]] champion [[Bozzie Baranta]].<ref name="Prima">[[Star Wars: Episode I Racer: Prima's Official Strategy Guide|''Star Wars'': Episode I ''Racer'': Prima's Official Strategy Guide]]</ref>

Eventually, one of Yunga's [[Cy Yunga's Podracer|Podracer]] [[Engine/Legends|engines]] ended up in the junkyard of a Tatooine-based [[Wald's Parts|parts shop]] owned by the [[Toydarian/Legends|Toydarian]] [[Watto/Legends|Watto]].<ref name="E1R" />

==Personality and traits==
Yunga was a member of a thin, long-necked alien species that exhibited [[Color/Legends|dark-green]] scales, lanky [[Limb/Legends|arms]], and short, stubby legs. Yunga's neck led to a single, large green [[Eye/Legends|eye]].<ref name="E1R" />

==Equipment==
Cy Yunga piloted a Podracer with a red, rocket ship-shaped [[Cockpit/Legends|cockpit]] and two thin red and white engines with a [[Comet/Legends|comet]]-esque design. The Podracer was fast and agile, yet was frail compared to some of its larger competition.<ref name="E1R" />

==Behind the scenes==
Cy Yunga was created for the [[Star Wars video games|video game]] ''[[Star Wars: Episode I Racer|Star Wars Episode I: Racer]]'' as one of the game's playable characters. Rather than appearing on the game's main roster, Yunga was instead treated as a bonus "cheat code" character that could be unlocked with the codes RRCYYUN or RRTANGENTABACUS in the [[Wikipedia:Nintendo 64|Nintendo 64]] version of the game and through a key combination in the PC version. Upon activation, Cy Yunga completely replaces "Bullseye" Navior, including Navior's records and placement as the track favorite of the Sunken City course. Cy Yunga also makes use of Navior's sound-bytes, and as such Yunga's voice, like Navior's, is provided by [[Bob Bergen]].<ref name="E1R" />

Cy Yunga's in-game model borrows the body from the model of the [[Glymphid/Legends|Glymphid]] pilot [[Aldar Beedo/Legends|Aldar Beedo]], but with a new texture.<ref name="E1R" />
